data.summary: This React component, StockChart, receives stock data as a prop and renders a responsive line chart using the Recharts library to visualize the closing prices of a specified company over time, displaying the company name, ticker symbol, and interactive chart elements such as tooltips and legends.

data.summary: This React component, StockChart, receives stock data as a prop, processes it to extract and format date and closing price information, and renders a responsive line chart using Recharts to visually display the stock's price history along with company name and ticker symbol.

data.summary: This code sets up an Express server with CORS and JSON parsing, defines a mock stock data generator, and provides two API endpoints: one that returns a simple greeting and another that returns either mock or real stock price data for a given symbol, defaulting to mock data, while listening on port 3000.

section_data.summaryData: This code sets up an Express server with CORS and JSON parsing, defines a mock stock data generator, and provides two API endpoints: one that returns a simple greeting and another that returns either mock or real stock price data for a given symbol, defaulting to mock data, while listening on port 3000.
section_data.title: Untitled

The frontend also wants to know the current stock price

section_data.summaryData: This code defines a React functional component called StockChart that takes stock data as a prop, formats the date and closing price for each data point, and renders a responsive line chart using the Recharts library to visually display the stock's price trend over time, along with chart features like tooltips and legends.
